message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step reasoning:

1) REG-030 (Art13(6)(iv)-(v)):
- Scope: Requires, where applicable/appropriate, disclosure of the high-risk AI system’s technical capabilities relevant to explaining outputs and its performance regarding specific persons/groups.
- Cross-references: None provided in the dataset for Art 13(6).
- Policy review: The chunk focuses on actions when intended use is not supported by evidence (remove intended use from materials, notify customers, revise/publish documentation, include info in Transparency Note, careful communications, legal guidance). It does not address or contradict requirements to disclose technical capabilities for explainability or performance across groups. As omissions are treated as Non-Deviation, no deviation detected.

2) REG-043 (Art26(1)):
- Scope: Deployers must take technical/organizational measures to ensure use in accordance with instructions for use, “pursuant to paragraphs 3 and 6.”
- Cross-references considered:
  • REG-045 (Art26(3)): Clarifies obligations under (1) and (2) are without prejudice to other obligations and allows deployers freedom to organize resources for implementing provider-indicated human oversight measures.
  • REG-048 (Art26(6)): Requires deployers to keep automatically generated logs under their control for at least six months, unless otherwise by law, with specific note for financial institutions.
- Policy review: The policy chunk does not discuss use in accordance with instructions for use, human oversight measures, or log retention. It provides a process for handling unsupported intended uses and communicating to customers. There is no conflict with Art26(1), nor with 26(3) or 26(6). Omissions are Non-Deviation.

3) REG-049 (Art26(7)):
- Scope: Before putting into service/using high-risk AI at the workplace, employers (as deployers) must inform workers’ representatives and affected workers, in line with applicable Union/national law/practice.
- Cross-references: None specified in the provided dataset for Art26(7).
- Policy review: The policy chunk does not mention workplace deployments or informing workers/representatives. No contradiction is present. Omissions are Non-Deviation.

Conclusion: No explicit contradictions, narrowings, or altered scopes versus the cited Articles (and references) are found in the provided policy chunk. All gaps are omissions, which per instruction must be treated as Non-Deviation.
</deliberation>
